reviews.review_description: I do not recommend this game. It is confusing and hard to play, costs too much for what y oh get, and I couldn’t even build anything, it was impossible to attach pieces to each other and I overall don’t like it.

This game got me into STEM and now my future plans are to become an engineer or something along those lines! After playing this game, I really got into learning about computer science, building and programming. It was something I never thought I would like, but it turns out I love it. (And keep in mind I got this game last year, and still play it today.) plus I’m only 14 so it really shows how fun the game is to actually keep you invested in learning and prototyping. I’m surprised this game isn’t in the charts, it should be.

DOWNLOAD IT!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for sharing this! Helping people discover their interests is the best possible outcome for the kinds of games we make, and hearing your story means the world to us. We look forward to seeing your legendary engineering career unfold!

reviews.review_description: This is a really good game. It is super fun and has no limit to its possibilities. There are some features I would like. One is add controller movement controls for mech builds. it detects controller up down left and right movements for controlling servos and stuff. If you see this please consider this!! I have so many fun ideas I could do with this!!!!

This is a superb game, and something that's a great example of what VR is about. Having tasks to solve, and building a robot right in front of you, working out how you can get something assembled to complete a task. Weighing your options as to what might set your robot back, etc.  Some of the building controls take a bit to get used to, but once you tinker with it you'll get it.

With that said, I do have two glaring negatives:

1.  The game requires an active internet connection to play (as labeled in the store). It does not appear to have any features that should require internet just to be able to launch the game. Times without internet are rare cases, I know. But if you owned a flashlight that wouldn't turn on without an internet connection, that would be quite intrusive and there is no difference to me in this case. So far, I can't tell why it's requiring an active connection.

2.  I think there was a missed opportunity with the sandbox mode. It would have been awesome to create your own objectives from a set of different types of levels where you can move things around, choose different objective types and set them up, etc. This would skyrocket the replay value and make it feel more like a virtual playground. As far as I can tell, the sandbox arena is a static area that you'll eventually grow bored of. This totally hampers the boundless possibilities because you'll run out of objectives to build robots for.

It's still a great game, but those 2 things really keep it from being a smash hit for me.
Such a fun and well executed game, I love building things and the physics are pretty accurate aswell. Blown away with how great this game is and the amount you can customize robots.

After trying out about 200 Quest games/apps I can say this is something special. Its high quality, innovative and fun. Also offers tens of hours (or even hundreds) of play time for a person who is intrested in building/testing/goofing.

The controls are super weird. Why do we need to grab stuff with triggers and not the grips? What is this, Meta Quest PCVR? Also, in the tutorial, when I finished Step 4, it wouldn't take me to Step 5. Absolutely rubbish! I would not recommend this to anyone who might have the same issue as I did!

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks for taking the time to share your experience with our robotics game, we're really sorry to hear it was frustrating. Your feedback about the controls is totally valid. We designed the grab mechanic around triggers to keep things consistent with certain gameplay interactions, but your input that we should consider other options has been noted for future development reference. As for the tutorial bug at Step 4, that definitely shouldn't be happening, so we've passed this along to our QA team for investigation. Thanks again for helping us get better. We hope you’ll give it another shot soon.

reviews.review_description:
I LOVE building robots, kit building, lego building. But storing all these kids has been terrible. I just don't have room. This gives me a fun creative place to play without needing to store all my pieces anywhere :)

Any thought of adding a pass through mode? I would LOVE to scan my room, then drive my robot around my living room!

Ok so that’s all of Saturday gone then. 
Amazing fun building robotics in vr. I like the challenges but must admit just building for fun is very distracting! 
A small bit of frustration removing prices and they almost disappear sometimes. All in all though fantastic.
They just stop developing the after 2 weeks I fun and has fun content but not much to do when it does not get updates
this app is great. it is creative and educational. more laguages should help schools
It’s a great game. There’s so many robots you can build on sandbox. If a developer sees this please take into consideration drone pieces.
The controls are a bit hart to figure out but besides that its awesome

Love this game! Really gets your mind working! The mechanics are spot on too! I love a good challenge and can seriously recommend this game for anyone who likes to build stuff, get creative, and like a challenge!

Looking forward to seeing any future updates that may come, I do hope there will be more levels.

Mixed Reality mode would be a perfect addition for sure! 

Great work Devs!

developer_response.body: Wow, thank you! It's fantastic that the game is challenging you and sparking your creativity - that's exactly what we aimed for! We're so glad you're enjoying the mechanics and the building aspects. We'll also definitely keep your suggestions in mind as we fine-tune the game. Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we're grateful for your support. Thanks again for your kind words and for taking the time to share your thoughts!

reviews.review_description: The game is overall just great and it couldn't be better in my opinion!

developer_response.body: We might be biased... but we agree. 🤜🤛

reviews.review_description:
I love building and designing things. got this so my son could learn basic engineering and how robotics and machines work. Would like to see more parts added like hover motors , artillery canopies ,and metallic skins to make flying robots.
And MR mode would be cool so i could play with my inventions in my house. This game has major replayability. Loving it.  
.........after digging in deeper.  I now have a DRONE ! AND . i can ride in it . AWESOME YALL

developer_response.body:
We’re so glad you love the game and are using it to explore your passion for building and designing! We're thrilled that Some Assembly Required is not only providing you with hours of creative fun but also serving as a tool to introduce your son to basic engineering and robotics concepts.

We really appreciate your suggestions for additional parts like hover motors, artillery canopies, and metallic skins. Our team is always looking for ways to expand the creative possibilities within the game, and your ideas align perfectly with our vision of enabling players to build their dream machines.

The idea of an MR mode that would allow you to interact with your creations in your own space is incredibly exciting! We'll definitely take note of this feature request.

And wow, a drone that you can ride in? That's seriously impressive! Thank you for taking the time to provide such thoughtful feedback and for supporting our game!

reviews.review_description: This is a game I'll be coming back to! Like all great games it pulls together many influences into a seamless whole: Besieged, Human Fall Flat, Bad Piggies, Fantastic Contraption, and more. It's very very polished! It makes me think and laugh at the same time, which is a total win.

developer_response.body:
We're thrilled that Some Assembly Required has earned a spot on your list of games to keep coming back to. It's an honor to be mentioned alongside such fantastic titles!

Your comment about the game making you think and laugh at the same time is incredibly encouraging, because that's exactly the experience we were aiming for!

Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts and for your support.

reviews.review_title: Always wanted a game like this
reviews.review_description: This hits my itch I’ve always had! I’m a mechanical engineer and am having a lot of fun with this! Great game!

developer_response.body: Hey we love to hear it! It means a lot to know that a real mechanical engineer is getting enjoyment out of our robotics sandbox. 🤖

reviews.review_title: Nice robot building game with potential.
reviews.review_description:
This game is basically a port of a PC game called RoboCo. And as a port of a PC game to VR goes, it does it pretty well! 
If you enjoyed the vehicle building physics games from PC then you will enjoy this as this game allows you to build vehicles and other mechanical contraptions. 
The game offers Campaign, Sandbox and a Robot Repair scenarios where you try to fix a pre-built along with learning how it works. Some Campaigns feature secret objectives to do to earn cosmetic parts for your robot. 
The game's editor is translated very well from PC to VR as it features similar tools to the PC version of the game but with the ability to pick up things using your controllers/hands. As the camera controls go, I wish they were improved. The only way to move your camera is by holding both grip buttons and i wish that you could just hold one grip to move around. There's also no way to turn off the zoom in/out window thing that always appears when you zoom which i find annoying. What's more annoying is that i wish i didn't accidentally zoom in/out when holding both grips and wish that zooming was something you would have to toggle with triggers while holding grips. The game could use some Mixed Reality mode of some sort. As i can imagine building something and seeing it move around in your real life room would be a nice thing, especially would be nice since Quest 3 was heavily advertised to feature better Mixed Reality compare to Quest 2.

Really hope this game will get more updates down the line as this is a great game for those who always wanted a building game of some sort in VR that allowed you to make vehicles and robots.

developer_response.body:
Our friend from Discord! Thank you for your detailed and insightful review of Some Assembly Required! We appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts and experiences with the game.

It's great to hear that you feel we've done a good job translating the vehicle-building physics gameplay from PC to VR. Our goal was to create an immersive and intuitive experience that captures the essence of the original game while leveraging the unique capabilities of VR.
We're also glad you're enjoying the various game modes, including the Campaign, Sandbox, and Robot Repair scenarios. The secret objectives in the Campaign mode are a fun way to unlock cosmetic parts and add an extra layer of challenge and replayability.

Your feedback on the camera controls and zoom functionality is invaluable. We're always looking for ways to improve the user experience, and your suggestions regarding single-grip camera movement and toggle-based zooming are great ideas. We'll definitely take them into consideration for future updates.

The idea of a Mixed Reality mode is really exciting. As you mentioned, with the enhanced MR capabilities of the Quest 3, there's a lot of potential for creating even more immersive and interactive experiences. We'll keep this in mind for the future!

Thank you again for your support and for sharing your thoughts. We're committed to making Some Assembly Required the best it can be, and feedback like yours is essential in helping us achieve that goal. Happy building!

reviews.review_description:
This game is so much fun and simple to play. Although there are definitely some bugs. I built a robot that can climb up walls but if I grab a block for too long they both go flying.

The play is so simple I skipped the tutorial and was still able to play right.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your kind words! We worked super hard to make Some Assembly Required accessible to as many players as possible, so it's really encouraging to hear that you found the game easy to learn. We're sorry about that bug you found - with a physics-driven experience like Some Assembly Required, there's a lot of sneaky little bugs like that to find and squish, but rest assured that we're on the case! We hope you continue to have a great time building bots - don't forget to share some of your brilliant creations on the in-game workshop, we'd love to see them!

reviews.review_description: from design to testing and innovation the process of building and making robots is so much fun but when I started I felt very frustrated with the controls because I skipped the totorial, I don't think that should even be an option, but eventually I figured out how to use the game and it truly is everything I hoped for and more the physics are so fun. The only thing I wish was the totorial is better, I showed it to my family and friends they understood the controls really quickly but only after I explained them so the controls are easy to use and understand but I feel they could be explained better at the beginning is all. Thank you!!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your awesome review and for sharing your experience with Some Assembly Required! It's fantastic to hear that you're having a blast designing, testing, and innovating with the game's robot-building mechanics. We're thrilled that it's living up to your expectations and providing hours of fun.

We also really appreciate your feedback about the tutorial and the initial learning curve. We're always looking for ways to improve the onboarding experience for new players, and we'll keep your thoughts in mind as we continue to fine-tune the introductory parts of the game.

It's awesome that you've been sharing the game with your family and friends, and that they were able to pick up the controls quickly once you explained them. Sounds like you're a great teacher!

Thank you again for your support and for taking the time to provide such thoughtful feedback!

reviews.review_description: This game is super fun and entertaining, there's sandbox, tutorials, or campaign modes. There are tons of parts and the controls are pretty simple and customizable

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review! We're happy that you're having such a fun and entertaining experience. It's fantastic to hear that you're enjoying the different game modes and finding the controls intuitive and customizable.
 
Thanks again for sharing your feedback and happy creating!

reviews.review_title: I think this is the first review I love
reviews.review_description: The game is so good I love to build robots
Amazing game
Looks like fun for someone with a creative mind. Also, fifth! 🙂
I love this game building robots is sooooo fun
Robots :3

When I bought this I had doubts but it's actually really fun and it's my brain to work

If you live robots and or sandbox games its a must buy

developer_response.body: We're so glad Some Assembly Required exceeded your expectations and you're having a blast with it! Your kind words mean a lot to us. Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts, and happy building!

reviews.review_description: Second Review
